author: claude
content:
## Implementation Plan

The test server (test/utilities.py) uses http.server.SimpleHTTPRequestHandler with a custom MyHandler that already supports special paths (e.g. /MAGIC_REDIRECT/). No secondary server needed.

### Approach
Add a /CONNECTION_ABORT/ path in MyHandler.do_GET that:
1. Sends response headers (200 OK, Content-Length: 99999)
2. Writes a few bytes of body
3. Forcibly closes the socket (self.wfile.close() or self.connection.close())

This simulates a server abort after headers are sent. onHeadersReceived fires (headers arrived), but onCompleted does NOT fire (body never completes).

### Test
In test/test_dns_instrument.py, add a test that:
1. Navigates to http://localhost:<port>/CONNECTION_ABORT/
2. Asserts a dns_responses row exists with populated ip/addresses
3. This test would FAIL on master (onCompleted never fires for aborted connections)

### Files to modify
- test/utilities.py — add /CONNECTION_ABORT/ handler in MyHandler.do_GET
- test/test_dns_instrument.py — add test_dns_captured_on_connection_abort

GH issue #1160 deleted (crosslink-only per user request).
